title: DNS resolves old IP after app migration

servicenow_seed.short_description: [TRAINING] App resolves to old IP after migration
servicenow_seed.description: Internal app crm.corp.local resolves to the old data-center IP for some users. App was migrated to a new subnet last night.

correct_steps: Verify caller and capture affected hostname (crm.corp.local)
Run nslookup crm.corp.local against client DNS and against authoritative DNS
Compare returned IP vs the post-migration IP
Check related incidents - others on same subnet may be hitting the same stale record
Capture TTL and the change window; escalate to Network Engineering to push the corrected record
rationale: Stale DNS after a migration is an infrastructure record problem, not a client problem. Capture nslookup evidence so Network Engineering can fix once.
hints: ipconfig /flushdns is a single-user workaround, not a fix.
Compare client DNS vs authoritative DNS to prove staleness.
learning_objectives: Use nslookup to capture DNS evidence
Tie incidents to a recent change window

tool_clues.change: CHG0009123 'CRM migration to subnet 10.40.7.0/24' completed 22:00 last night.
